top_candidates.reasoning: The 'considering also your own duty (svadharma)' framing — naming the role-specific-accumulated-context as the operative thing, plus the 'api' (also/even) marking it as a second independent argument — is the structural signature of svadharmam api cāvekṣya na vikampitum arhasi. The kṣatriyasya na vidyate (none other exists for the kṣatriya) is preserved in the 'no nobler escape into a cleaner problem than the dharmic problem the role itself has handed you' clause.

Svadharma also appears in 18.47 (śreyān svadharmo viguṇaḥ), but 18.47 contrasts svadharma with another's well-performed dharma — a different doctrinal point. 2.31 names the role's accumulated context and the dharma-yuddha-as-highest-good; 18.47 names svadharma's superiority even when imperfectly performed.

Also a svadharma verse (śreyān svadharmo viguṇaḥ paradharmāt svanuṣṭhitāt) — but 3.35 emphasises preferring imperfect svadharma over well-performed paradharma, while 2.31 emphasises the role-context as the source of obligation. Different argument structure.
